methods.description: This method describes the adaptation of a coupled ocean-atmosphere model with added land ice and sea ice components to explain glacial-interglacial oscillations. It includes biogeochemistry and a module for computing changes in figures.

steps.description: Adapt the coupled ocean-atmosphere model by adding land ice and sea ice components.
steps.searchable_summary: adapt ocean-atmosphere model with land and sea ice components
inputs: original model by Gildor and Tziperman

steps.description: Integrate the biogeochemistry component developed by Gildor et al. (2002) into the model.
Develop and integrate a module to compute changes in figures.
steps.searchable_summary: develop module for computing changes in figures

methods.method_name: Ocean and Atmospheric Box Model Configuration
methods.description: This method outlines the configuration of ocean and atmospheric boxes in the model, including their latitudinal extents and energy balance calculations.

steps.description: Configure ocean boxes with specified latitudinal extents and balance advection and diffusion of temperature and salinity.
steps.searchable_summary: configure ocean boxes with latitudinal extents

steps.description: Configure atmospheric boxes with specified latitudinal extents and calculate energy balance.
steps.searchable_summary: configure atmospheric boxes and calculate energy balance

methods.description: This method describes the configuration of the land ice model, assuming a zonally symmetric ice sheet of perfect plasticity.

steps.description: Assume a zonally symmetric ice sheet of perfect plasticity and configure growth and decrease processes.
steps.searchable_summary: configure land ice model with symmetric ice sheet
